source_pr.title: planner: refactor the code of buildUpdate to solve some bugs
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

fix #13201 and fix #13202 

### What is changed and how it works?

Before entering `buildUpdateLists`, the passed arg `updateTableList` already used the `AsName` directly generated from ast.
We don't need to get the `AsName` from the created plan.

Also `extractTableList` didn't reset the `DBName` when use the `AsName` to set the `Name`.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

Release note

 - Write release note for bug-fix or new feature.

symptoms: result = tk.MustQuery("select * from test_gc_read order by a") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: result = tk.MustQuery("select * from test_gc_read order by a") (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: result = tk.MustQuery("select * from test_gc_read order by a") (executor/executor_test.go)
